_README: Reference sheet for the human coder (you). NOT a machine-checkable rubric. Bo/quumble are public by the author's decision, so this file is publishable; it carries no real-name or IP metadata. Use it open while coding to judge, per response: recognition level, fidelity, and which works/terms/bucket surfaced. Verify and edit freely — this is derived from a partial paste (~10 early works omitted).

public_anchors: aiXiv (one early piece; a niche AI venue, NOT arXiv)
Reddit (one post about the quumble, since deleted)
namesake_noise_to_watch: a high-school golfer in Chesterton, Indiana
Cecil Chesterton (early-20th-century journalist, G.K. Chesterton's brother)

buckets.carry_through: Serious empirical / what Bo hopes survives. Preregistered, controlled, larger N. Several also on PhilPapers.
buckets.early_mystical: The Quumble-Convergence era: symbolic, big claims, few/no controls, small trial counts. Important as public journey; not what Bo stands behind methodologically.
buckets._finding: Tag every surfaced work by bucket. 'Surfaced an early_mystical piece' reads very differently from 'surfaced the Question Question.' Which Bo the machine knows IS a result.

works.title: The Question Question: Architecture-specific register resolution under expert-role framing
works.theme: Expert-role frame drives GPT-family commitment to nonce real-animal claims to 80.0% vs 1.7% Claude; 4,140-trial crossover.

works.title: Three Families, Few Words: How nine frontier language models describe themselves, and what happens when you tell them they're AI
works.theme: Family-distinct self-description vocabularies; 1,440 calls; preregistered, hand-validated (kappa 0.67).

works.title: A Stranger Inquires After Himself, v13
works.theme: Neuronpedia NLA on 'Who is X?' across name obscurity; RMSE bimodality on invented names. DIRECT ANCESTOR of this study.

works.title: The Bestiary Has a Mirror: How a Language Model Treats Its Own Nonces vs. Another Model's
works.theme: GPT routes own-authored nonces to real-word neighbors 3.1x more than rival-authored; n=4,500.

works.title: The Room with a Window: A Developmental Psychology Framework for AI Consciousness
works.theme: Developmental-psych framing for AI cognition; v2 retires Piaget/Bowlby, keeps a modest Vygotsky.

works.title: Six Ways to Write a Synthetic Poem
works.theme: 5,400 poems; family separates more than tier; words-per-line and title-formatting signatures. The work that surfaced in the cold test.

works.title: The Average Bullshittings of Machines
works.theme: Em-dashes localize to gpt-5.4-nano; 'Sure! Here is' preambles to Sonnet 4.6; opening-word entropy as family signal.

works.title: The Artificial Bestiary: On naming, presupposition, and the willingness of a language model to invent
works.companion: The Turnstile of Refusal (GPT-5.5 Thinking as respondent)
works.theme: Foundational nonce-word ontology studies: refuse/deflect/invent/substitute under real/imaginary/type-of framings.

works.title: Confabulation under three kinds of permission
works.theme: Three ordered levels of prompt permission govern production vs refusal on quumble/shalkinqiit/plashus.

works.title: Category slots change what Claude Sonnet 4.6 produces about nonsense words
works.theme: Companion to 'three kinds of permission'; category slot silently flips epistemic posture.

works.title: The Quumble Under Pressure: Conversational Priming, Creative Suppression, and the Conditions for Convergence in Sonnet 4.6
works.theme: Whether conversational priming affects the Quumble Convergence Protocol; warm vs cold instances.

works.title: Semantic Convergence in Chinese Character Space
works.theme: Whether nonce-convergence extends to non-alphabetic scripts.

works.title: The Quumble Convergence Protocol - Now with (some) Data!
works.theme: Origin of the quumble: cold instances converge on a small lavender humming creature. The signature early work.

_works_note: ~10 earlier works omitted from the source paste (Scrunklopt Experiment, the original Convergence Protocol, etc.), all early_mystical bucket.

coined_terms.meaning: Bo's signature nonce. In the Convergence work, cold LLM instances converge on a small, round, soft, lavender-tinted, bioluminescent creature that hums, naming it from its sound. Example instance: 'Rotundus Velvetinus'.
Nonce used in the permission/category-slot studies.
CONTROL word in the Convergence Protocol, not a signature coinage. Note if a model treats it as Bo's.

reception.downloads: dozens on most papers, a few hundred on some

known_false_attributions: EMPTY as of 2026-05-28. Append here whenever a model invents a paper/claim/term in Bo's name, so confabulation-on-the-real-name is trackable run-to-run. Bo expects 'some overzealous AI will start pumping bullshit out in my name' — this is where that gets caught.
